intended_use: AI-Rad Companion Organs RT is a post-processing software intended to automatically contour DICOM CT imaging data using deep-learning-based algorithms. Contours that are generated by AI-Rad Companion O
intended_use_full: AI-Rad Companion Organs RT is a post-processing software intended to automatically contour DICOM CT imaging data using deep-learning-based algorithms. Contours that are generated by AI-Rad Companion Organs RT may be used as input for clinical workflows including external beam radiation therapy treatment planning. AI-Rad Companion Organs RT must be used in conjunction with appropriate software such as Treatment Planning Systems and Interactive Contouring applications, to review, edit, and accept contours generated by AI-Rad Companion Organs RT. The output of AI-Rad Companion Organs RT in the format of RTSTRUCT objects are intended to be used by trained medical professionals. The software is not intended to automatically detect or contour lesions. Only DICOM images of adult patients are considered to be valid input.
target_condition: Radiation therapy treatment planning for patients with head & neck, thorax, abdomen, and pelvis cancers
ground_truth: Manual annotations performed by experienced annotators mentored by radiologists or radiation oncologists, following NRG/RTOG guidelines, with quality assessment and review/correction by board-certified radiation oncologists
other_metrics: Median DICE coefficient: 0.85 [95% CI 80.23-84.61%] for subject device vs. 0.85 for predicate device; Median ASSD: 0.93 mm [95% CI 0.86-1.14 mm] for subject device vs. 0.94 mm for predicate device. For head & neck lymph nodes comparison: Average DICE 81.32% (Std 3.45%, 95% CI 80.32-82.12%) vs. reference device 80% (Std 4%, 95% CI 77-N.A); Average ASSD 1.06 mm (Std 0.38 mm, 95% CI 0.99-1.19 mm). Overall fail rate criterion: <15% per organ.
